question: Which current tools are closest to a browser-triggered, local-first, work-object anchored direct resume tool for Codex/Claude sessions?

items.name: iloom
items.closest_gap: Persists plans/analysis/decisions to the tracker and creates isolated work environments, but does not appear to use the browser work-object URL as a direct resume trigger for reopening the exact local prior session.

items.name: Wormhole
items.category: browser URL to local workspace routing
items.closest_gap: Very close on browser URL to local context. Adds buttons on JIRA/GitHub pages to jump to task workspaces/tmux/editor, but not to recover the exact prior Codex/Claude session.

items.name: CodeRabbit Issue Planner
items.closest_gap: Issue-anchored planning and handoff, not direct local session recovery.

items.name: Codex in Linear
items.closest_gap: Lets users run Codex tasks from Linear issues, but this is issue-native agent execution, not local direct resume into a prior session.

items.name: Claude Code memory
items.closest_gap: Strengthens repo/project memory inside Claude Code, which makes generic repo-memory less differentiated, but it is still not work-object anchored direct resume.

items.name: Memdex
items.closest_gap: Closer on cross-tool saved context than on work-object anchored direct local session recovery.

why: The phrase matches the actual differentiator.
Technical suffixes reduce SEO/package ambiguity from generic resume terms.
Connector package names make the architecture obvious without exposing a broad platform story to users.
